instruments: Joint MAS/Deutsche Bundesbank media release, 'MAS and Deutsche Bundesbank sign MoU on tokenisation and cross-border settlement', 13 November 2025 -- fetched as a genuine PDF via sgpc.gov.sg (Singapore Press Centre), since www.mas.gov.sg served an identical 853,817-byte 'Maintenance' bot-block page to this project's honest User-Agent, plain curl and WebFetch alike on every path tried this session (verified 2026-07-13), including a direct -/media/ PDF path tested for this pillar (the Global Layer One whitepaper). The release's own footnote states verbatim: 'Project Guardian was launched by MAS in May 2022' -- the only primary-sourced confirmation of Project Guardian's launch date obtained this session -- and separately notes the Deutsche Bundesbank has been a member of the Guardian Policymaker Group since November 2024. The MoU itself, signed by MAS Deputy Managing Director (Markets and Development) Leong Sing Chiong and Deutsche Bundesbank Executive Board member Burkhard Balz on the sidelines of the Singapore FinTech Festival, commits the two central banks to develop cross-border settlement solutions and promote common standards for tokenised-asset payments, FX and securities flows between Singapore and Germany, and describes the partnership as building on Project Guardian, characterised in the release as a collaborative initiative between policymakers and the financial industry to enhance liquidity and efficiency in financial markets through asset tokenisation

MAS media release, 'MAS Announces Plans to Support Commercialisation of Asset Tokenisation', 4 November 2024 -- fetched as a genuine PDF via sgpc.gov.sg; states that MAS has, under Project Guardian, 'convened over 40 financial institutions, industry associations and international policymakers across seven jurisdictions' to carry out industry trials on asset tokenisation in capital markets, with more than 15 industry trials conducted to date across six currencies and multiple financial products (funds, structured products, fixed income and foreign exchange); announces formation of the Guardian Wholesale Network industry group (named participants: Citi, HSBC, Schroders, Standard Chartered and UOB) to commercialise tokenisation trials on a coordinated, multi-member network basis; announces the Global Layer One (GL1) initiative -- launched in 2023 to develop shared ledger infrastructure for tokenised assets, led by a core group of banks (BNY, Citi, J.P. Morgan, MUFG and Societe Generale-FORGE) -- is expanding to develop Control Principles, technical Specifications and Compliance-by-Design templates for cross-border interoperability, with Euroclear and HSBC joining as new participants; publishes two industry frameworks developed by Project Guardian industry group members, the Guardian Fixed Income Framework (GFIF, integrating ICMA's Bond Data Taxonomy, the Capital Markets and Technology Association's Token Standards and GFMA's Design Principles for Tokenised Securities) and the Guardian Funds Framework (GFF, including the Guardian Composable Token Taxonomy for tokenised investment vehicles); and announces the SGD Testnet, an initial wholesale CBDC test network offering S$ wholesale CBDC issuance/transfer/redemption, programmability (including a Purpose Bound Money feature), and interoperability with existing market infrastructure, available to eligible financial institutions in Project Guardian and Project Orchid, with the first participating institutions named as DBS, OCBC, Standard Chartered and UOB

MAS media release, 'MAS Expands Industry Collaboration to Scale Asset Tokenisation for Financial Services', 27 June 2024 -- fetched as a genuine PDF via sgpc.gov.sg; states MAS had, over the preceding two years, worked with 24 financial institutions (asset managers, market operators, custodians, credit rating agencies and commercial banks) to pilot asset tokenisation use cases under Project Guardian, and announces the Global Financial Markets Association (GFMA), the International Capital Market Association (ICMA) and the International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA) as the first global industry associations to join Project Guardian's industry group, standing up three workstreams (Fixed Income, FX, and Asset & Wealth Management) to develop cross-jurisdiction standards; also announces successful completion of the first phase of the Global Layer One (GL1) initiative and publication of a GL1 whitepaper the same day, describing GL1's next phase as potentially including a non-profit GL1 Org to hold common principles, policies and standards for a global shared ledger infrastructure, with international policymakers (staff from the European Central Bank, Banque de France and the IMF) observing; quotes Mr Leong Sing Chiong, MAS Deputy Managing Director (Markets and Development): 'Project Guardian provides a useful platform for central banks, regulators and financial institutions'

MAS media release, 'MAS Proposes Framework for Digital Asset Networks', 26 June 2023 -- fetched as a genuine PDF via sgpc.gov.sg; announces publication of the report 'Enabling Open & Interoperable Networks', jointly developed with the Bank for International Settlements' Committee on Payments and Market Infrastructures (CPMI) with contributions from DBS Bank, JP Morgan, HSBC, SBI Digital Asset Holdings, Standard Chartered and UOB, considering how CPMI-IOSCO principles for financial market infrastructures apply to digital asset networks; announces expansion of Project Guardian via a newly established Project Guardian Industry Group comprising 11 financial institutions running pilots in asset & wealth management, fixed income and FX (illustrative pilots described in the release include a digitally native structured-product pilot by HSBC, Marketnode and UOB; a UBS Asset Management pilot on native issuance of a Variable Capital Company fund; and a Standard Chartered/Linklogis pilot issuing asset-backed security tokens listed on the Singapore Exchange); and announces the Japan Financial Services Agency (JFSA) as the first overseas financial regulator to join Project Guardian; quotes Mr Leong Sing Chiong: 'While MAS strongly discourages and seeks to restrict speculation in cryptocurrencies'

Securities and Futures Act 2001 (SFA) and Payment Services Act 2019 (PS Act) -- the underlying statutory classification framework that determines which licensing/conduct regime, if any, applies to a specific tokenised instrument (SFA for a token that is a 'digital representation of capital markets products'; PS Act for a token that is a digital payment token), per MAS's own 6 June 2025 media release quoted in this jurisdiction's Dealing, Custody, Advisory & Management pillar card; Singapore has not enacted, and this research found no evidence MAS has proposed, a dedicated tokenisation- or RWA-specific licensing statute distinct from that existing classification framework -- Project Guardian and its associated frameworks (GFIF, GFF, GL1) are MAS-convened industry-collaboration and infrastructure-standards initiatives, not a licensing regime, consistent with this pillar's 'no_dedicated_regime' status_seal; the SFA/PS Act licensing mechanics themselves are tracked in the Exchanges (VATP) and Dealing, Custody, Advisory & Management pillars, not restated here
standing_summary: Singapore has no dedicated tokenisation or real-world-asset (RWA) licensing statute. A tokenised instrument's regulatory treatment instead follows the same technology-neutral classification framework tracked in this jurisdiction's Exchanges (VATP) and Dealing, Custody, Advisory & Management pillars: the Securities and Futures Act 2001 where the token is a 'digital representation of capital markets products', or the Payment Services Act 2019 where it is a digital payment token. What sits specifically in this pillar is Project Guardian, MAS's collaborative initiative with the financial industry -- confirmed, via a footnote in MAS and the Deutsche Bundesbank's own joint 13 November 2025 media release (fetched as a genuine PDF via the Singapore Press Centre, sgpc.gov.sg, since www.mas.gov.sg serves an identical bot-blocked 'Maintenance' page on every path tried this session including a direct GL1-whitepaper PDF path), to have been 'launched by MAS in May 2022' -- to test and, increasingly, commercialise asset tokenisation and digital-asset-network infrastructure for wholesale financial markets. By MAS's own account in its 4 November 2024 media release (also fetched via sgpc.gov.sg), Project Guardian has 'convened over 40 financial institutions, industry associations and international policymakers across seven jurisdictions', running more than 15 industry trials in six currencies across funds, structured products, fixed income and FX; an earlier 27 June 2024 release put the pilot-institution count at 24 financial institutions over the preceding two years, joined that month by the Global Financial Markets Association (GFMA), International Capital Market Association (ICMA) and International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA) as its first global industry-association members, standing up Fixed Income, FX, and Asset & Wealth Management workstreams. Regulatory participation has also grown: MAS's 26 June 2023 release welcomed Japan's Financial Services Agency (JFSA) as the first overseas financial regulator to join Project Guardian, and the November 2025 Bundesbank MoU confirms the Deutsche Bundesbank joined the Guardian Policymaker Group in November 2024 before signing a bilateral MoU on cross-border tokenised-asset settlement standards with MAS in November 2025. Three concrete workstream outputs sit under Project Guardian, all confirmed via direct fetch of MAS's own 4 November 2024 media release: the Guardian Fixed Income Framework (GFIF), integrating ICMA's Bond Data Taxonomy, the Capital Markets and Technology Association's Token Standards and GFMA's Design Principles for Tokenised Securities into an industry guide for tokenising debt capital markets instruments; the Guardian Funds Framework (GFF), including a Guardian Composable Token Taxonomy for tokenised investment vehicles; and the Global Layer One (GL1) initiative, launched in 2023 to develop shared ledger infrastructure on which commercial tokenised-asset networks can be deployed, led by a core group of banks (BNY, Citi, J.P. Morgan, MUFG and Societe Generale-FORGE per MAS's own release) with a whitepaper published 27 June 2024 and, from November 2024, an expanded scope covering cross-border Control Principles, technical Specifications and Compliance-by-Design templates, joined by Euroclear and HSBC as new participants. On the commercialisation side, MAS's November 2024 release announced the Guardian Wholesale Network, an industry group formed by Citi, HSBC, Schroders, Standard Chartered and UOB to commercialise their respective tokenisation trials on a coordinated multi-member network, and the SGD Testnet, an initial wholesale central bank digital currency (CBDC) test network offering issuance, transfer and redemption of Singapore-dollar wholesale CBDC, programmability including MAS's 'Purpose Bound Money' concept, and interoperability with existing financial market infrastructure -- made available to eligible financial institutions participating in Project Guardian and Project Orchid (this jurisdiction's wholesale-CBDC workstream, tracked in the Banking & Money pillar), with DBS, OCBC, Standard Chartered and UOB named as the first participating institutions. Mr Leong Sing Chiong, MAS's Deputy Managing Director (Markets and Development), is quoted across these releases articulating the initiative's posture: in June 2023, 'While MAS strongly discourages and seeks to restrict speculation in cryptocurrencies', signalling that Project Guardian's asset-tokenisation focus is deliberately distinguished from MAS's separate, restrictive posture on retail cryptocurrency speculation tracked in the Exchanges (VATP) pillar; and in June 2024, that 'Project Guardian provides a useful platform for central banks, regulators and financial institutions' to jointly understand tokenisation's opportunities and risks. Because none of this rests on a licensing statute -- MAS's own 6 June 2025 media release (cited in the Dealing, Custody, Advisory & Management pillar card) confirms tokenised instruments continue to be regulated under the existing SFA/PS Act/FSM Act framework according to what the token itself represents, not under any Project-Guardian-specific rule -- this pillar's status_seal is recorded as 'no_dedicated_regime': Singapore tokenises real-world and financial assets through MAS-convened voluntary industry collaboration and shared infrastructure-building, not through a dedicated tokenisation license or statute.

key_links.label: Singapore Press Centre (sgpc.gov.sg): Joint MAS/Deutsche Bundesbank media release, 'MAS and Deutsche Bundesbank sign MoU on tokenisation and cross-border settlement', 13 November 2025 -- official PDF, source of the Project Guardian May-2022 launch-date footnote; fetch-verified live this session

Singapore Press Centre (sgpc.gov.sg): MAS media release, 'MAS Announces Plans to Support Commercialisation of Asset Tokenisation', 4 November 2024 -- official PDF, source of the GFIF/GFF/GL1-expansion/SGD Testnet/Guardian Wholesale Network facts; fetch-verified live this session

Singapore Press Centre (sgpc.gov.sg): MAS media release, 'MAS Expands Industry Collaboration to Scale Asset Tokenisation for Financial Services', 27 June 2024 -- official PDF, source of the GL1 whitepaper announcement and GFMA/ICMA/ISDA workstream facts; fetch-verified live this session

Singapore Press Centre (sgpc.gov.sg): MAS media release, 'MAS Proposes Framework for Digital Asset Networks', 26 June 2023 -- official PDF, source of the Enabling Open & Interoperable Networks report and JFSA-joins-Project-Guardian facts; fetch-verified live this session

MAS: Project Guardian scheme/initiative overview page -- link only; www.mas.gov.sg served the same 853,817-byte 'Maintenance' bot-block page to this project's honest User-Agent, plain curl and WebFetch alike this session, verified 2026-07-13; cannot currently satisfy the quote-authenticity gate

MAS: Global Layer One (GL1) Whitepaper, May 2024 -- link only; -/media/ PDF path on www.mas.gov.sg served the same 'Maintenance' bot-block page this session (verified directly against this exact URL), despite the whitepaper's existence and 27 June 2024 publication date being independently confirmed via MAS's own sgpc.gov.sg-hosted media release

MAS: Guardian Fixed Income Framework (GFIF), November 2024 -- link only; www.mas.gov.sg blocked this session; GFIF's existence, publication date and content are instead confirmed via MAS's own sgpc.gov.sg-hosted 4 November 2024 media release

MAS: Guardian Funds Framework (GFF), November 2024 -- link only; www.mas.gov.sg blocked this session; GFF's existence, publication date and content are instead confirmed via MAS's own sgpc.gov.sg-hosted 4 November 2024 media release

MAS: 'Guide on the Tokenisation of Capital Markets Products' -- link only; www.mas.gov.sg blocked this session; relevant to SFA applicability for tokenised capital-markets products (the classification backbone this pillar's 'no_dedicated_regime' seal rests on) but not independently fetched as primary text

open_items: The original Project Guardian launch press release ('MAS Partners the Industry to Pilot Use Cases in Digital Assets', reported May 2022) was not independently located and fetched at a working sgpc.gov.sg PDF URL this session -- its title was found only via search-engine indexing of the blocked www.mas.gov.sg page. The May 2022 launch date used in this card instead rests on a direct-fetched primary source: a footnote in MAS and the Deutsche Bundesbank's own joint 13 November 2025 media release, which states verbatim 'Project Guardian was launched by MAS in May 2022'. Should the original 2022 release become independently fetchable (e.g. via sgpc.gov.sg), it should be fetched and cited directly.

The GL1 Whitepaper, Guardian Fixed Income Framework and Guardian Funds Framework PDF documents themselves were not independently fetched this session -- every www.mas.gov.sg -/media/ PDF path tried, including these three specific document URLs, served the same 853,817-byte 'Maintenance' bot-block page as MAS's general website. This card's account of their content rests on MAS's own descriptions of them inside its sgpc.gov.sg-hosted 27 June 2024 and 4 November 2024 media releases, not on the framework/whitepaper documents' own text. Should mas.gov.sg become fetchable, these three documents should be independently fetched and quoted directly.

The 4 November 2024 media release's cumulative figures (over 40 financial institutions, industry associations and policymakers across seven jurisdictions; more than 15 industry trials in six currencies) are the most recent MAS-stated aggregate scale figures for Project Guardian found this session; no more recent MAS media release specifically restating or updating these totals was located. The 13 November 2025 Deutsche Bundesbank MoU confirms the initiative and the Guardian Policymaker Group's growth (Bundesbank joining November 2024) but does not restate the trial/institution counts, so this card does not claim the November 2024 figures are still current as of this pillar's generation date -- they are dated to their source release.

This research did not locate a Singapore Gazette or Securities and Futures Act amendment specifically addressing asset tokenisation as a standalone legal category -- consistent with the Guide on the Tokenisation of Capital Markets Products (link-only above, blocked on www.mas.gov.sg this session) and MAS's own media releases describing Project Guardian as industry collaboration rather than rulemaking, this card's 'no_dedicated_regime' status_seal should be revisited if MAS enacts or proposes dedicated tokenisation-specific legislation or a Guardian-branded licensing framework.

This pillar does not restate the Securities and Futures Act / Payment Services Act classification mechanics that determine a tokenised instrument's licensing treatment (dealt with in the Exchanges (VATP) and Dealing, Custody, Advisory & Management pillars) or MAS's wholesale-CBDC workstream under Project Orchid, which underpins the SGD Testnet's central-bank-money leg and is tracked in the Banking & Money pillar -- not duplicated here to avoid cross-pillar citation duplication.

This card names financial institutions (e.g. the Guardian Wholesale Network's Citi/HSBC/Schroders/Standard Chartered/UOB, and GL1's core banks BNY/Citi/J.P. Morgan/MUFG/Societe Generale-FORGE) only because MAS's own media releases name them as the direct subject of the initiatives being described (who convened or joined a named industry group), not as licensees, applicants or enforcement targets -- consistent with this project's named-entity rule, which governs commentary on regulated firms, not factual attribution of who a regulator's own release says formed a named voluntary industry consortium; no assessment of any named institution's product or conduct is offered.
